nodes.context: Colombian drug trafficking cartel formed in Medellín in the late 1990s, breakaway from Juárez Cartel.
Mexican drug cartel from which La Empresa broke away; members became adversaries.
Alliance of Juárez members and drug lords from Chihuahua, Durango, and Sinaloa formed in early 2000s.
Criminal group listed as ally of La Empresa in infobox.
Criminal group listed as ally of La Empresa in infobox.
Mexican drug cartel listed as rival of La Empresa in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel is alleged to be a "breakaway" cartel of the Juárez Cartel , many of the Juárez Cartel members became adversaries of the cartel
edges.context: La Empresa broke away from Juárez Cartel, and Juárez members became adversaries.

edges.evidence_quote: many of the Juárez Cartel members became adversaries of the cartel
edges.context: Juárez Cartel members became adversaries of La Empresa.

edges.evidence_quote: During the early 2000s, the Juarez members and drug lords from contiguous Mexican states forged an alliance that became known as 'The Golden Triangle Alliance' ... La Empresa formed during this time and was one of the gangs.
edges.context: La Empresa was one of the gangs that formed along with the alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Juárez Cartel , La Linea , Barrio Azteca
edges.context: La Linea is listed as an ally of La Empresa in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Juárez Cartel , La Linea , Barrio Azteca
edges.context: Barrio Azteca is listed as an ally of La Empresa in the infobox.
Sinaloa Cartel is listed as a rival of La Empresa in the infobox.
